abstraction_and_responsibility.reason: Entities (Paddle, Ball, Game) are well-encapsulated with clear single responsibilities. Each class handles its own data, movement, and drawing. Game class orchestrates interactions appropriately. Minor issue: collision logic could be more self-contained in Ball or a dedicated collider.

changeability_and_testability.reason: Constants are centralized, making tuning easy. Game rules are spread across multiple methods (check_collisions, check_scoring, check_winner), making targeted changes harder. No separation of pure logic from pygame rendering, making unit testing difficult without mocking entire display.

comment_usefulness.reason: Most comments are either redundant (restating obvious code) or trivial translations. They add little value to understanding intent or logic. Example: '上下边界反弹' simply repeats what the code clearly does.

semantic_readability.reason: Variable and method names are descriptive and follow consistent patterns (e.g., move_up, reset_position). Game logic flow in run() is linear and understandable. Some long methods (check_winner, draw_game_over) and mixed levels of abstraction slightly reduce readability.
